title: HardBoundarySpec v1
description: Machine-readable declaration of a dot.ink hard boundary configuration: what egress is permitted, which principals may use credentials, and where secrets live. This document is SHA-256 hashed and its digest is bound into every DecisionReceipt as hard_boundary_digest. The hash binds decisions to the exact boundary in force when they were made. A changed boundary (new allowed_cidrs, new secrets_provider, etc.) produces a different digest, which the verifier detects. STRICT allOf rules reject obviously insecure configurations at parse time.

boundary_version.description: SemVer for this boundary specification. Increment on any change. Old versions remain valid for verifying historical receipts.

profile.description: Deployment profile this boundary applies to.

issued_at.description: When this boundary spec was created.

egress.description: Network egress controls. STRICT requires default_deny=true.

default_deny.description: All outbound traffic denied unless in allowed_hostnames or allowed_cidrs. Must be true in ENTERPRISE and STRICT.

allowed_hostnames.description: Explicit allowlist of hostnames reachable from agent workloads. dot.ink gateway endpoint must be here.

allowed_cidrs.description: CIDR blocks reachable from agent workloads (e.g. VPC CIDR for dot.ink gateway).

dns.description: DNS resolver controls. Uncontrolled DNS allows exfil via DNS tunnel and hostname rebinding.

enforce_controlled_resolver.description: Agent workloads may only query the controlled resolver(s) in resolver_ips.

allow_external_dns.description: If true, workloads may query 8.8.8.8 or similar. Must be false in STRICT.

resolver_ips.description: IP addresses of the controlled DNS resolver(s).

identity.description: Identity and access controls. STRICT requires workload identity and no direct tool access.

workload_identity_required.description: Agent workloads must present a workload identity (IRSA, Workload Identity, SPIFFE) — no static API keys in workloads.

gateway_principal.description: IAM principal that the dot.ink gateway presents to tool/provider APIs (e.g. ARN, service account). Only this principal may decrypt secrets and call adapters.

tool_access.description: Controls for workload-to-tool and gateway-to-adapter communication paths.

deny_direct_tool_access_from_workloads.description: IAM policy denies all tool/provider API calls from workload identity — only gateway_principal may call them. Must be true in STRICT.

require_mtls_workload_to_gateway.description: mTLS required on the workload → gateway path. Prevents unauthenticated bypass even if egress filter is misconfigured.

require_mtls_gateway_to_adapters.description: mTLS required on the gateway → adapter path. Must be true in STRICT.

sidecar_interceptor_deployed.description: Pattern 2 sidecar proxy deployed. If true, P2_SIDECAR_TOKEN_ENFORCEMENT test is mandatory in boundary validation.

secrets.description: Secrets management controls. STRICT prohibits any secret in workload env/volumes.

secrets_provider.description: Where secrets are stored. NONE_DEV_ONLY is rejected in STRICT by allOf rule.

keys_in_workloads_forbidden.description: No API keys, signing keys, or credentials are injected into workload env vars, volumes, or metadata. Must be true in STRICT.

decrypt_allowed_principals.description: IAM principals permitted to decrypt secrets. Should match gateway_principal only in STRICT.

required.description: Key rotation is enforced (automated or procedural).

max_key_age_days.description: Maximum permitted age of any secret in days. 90 for ENTERPRISE, 30 for STRICT.

allOf.description: STRICT: egress default_deny must be true.
STRICT: external DNS must be disabled.
STRICT: direct tool access from workloads must be denied.
